headline: 1 deployed pool address identified; Aave Horizon RWA runs as separate V3.3 instance on Ethereum

findings.text: Block-explorer name-tag search: Etherscan manually searched but systematic enumeration incomplete. Pool address 0xAe05Cd22df81871bc7cC2a04BeCfb516bFe332C8 identified via documentation rather than block explorer tags.

Official deployments doc: https://aave.com/docs/resources/addresses lists V3/V4 core deployments but lacks dedicated Horizon section. Pool address documented in Horizon-specific docs at https://aave.com/docs/aave-v3/horizon.

Audit PDFs: V3.3 audits listed at https://aave.com/security. Direct GitHub audit folder access (https://github.com/aave/aave-v3-horizon/tree/main/audits) blocked; scope cannot be verified from audit PDFs.
GitHub deployment artifacts: Repository https://github.com/aave/aave-v3-horizon exists but direct access blocked. Foundry/hardhat deployments not accessible.
Multi-chain enumeration: Protocol.chains contains Ethereum only. Single chain deployment.
Factory-discovered children: No factory contracts identified in available sources.
Role taxonomy: Pool identified; RwaATokenManager mentioned in documentation for RWA-specific aToken administrative functions.
Ratchet output integrity: Pool address 0xAe05Cd22df81871bc7cC2a04BeCfb516bFe332C8 traced to https://aave.com/docs/aave-v3/horizon documentation.

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 1 contract address (Aave Horizon Pool) from documented sources. Horizon is a separate V3.3 instance with RWA-specific extensions. GitHub repo blocked; audit scope, PoolAddressesProvider, PoolConfigurator, and other supporting contracts cannot be enumerated without direct access. RwaATokenManager role identified but address not yet discovered.

evidence.shows: Aave Horizon Pool address 0xAe05Cd22df81871bc7cC2a04BeCfb516bFe332C8 documented in React integration example; market is separate V3.3 instance for RWAs
Aave Horizon is a lending market built on Aave Protocol v3.3 for RWA collateral and stablecoin borrowing; launched with Circle, Superstate, and Centrifuge RWA assets
Official addresses dashboard lists V3 core market and V4 deployments but no dedicated Horizon section; RwaATokenManager referenced in tokenization docs as managing RWA-specific aToken functions
RwaAToken and RwaATokenManager contracts documented as managing non-transferable RWA collateral aTokens; ATOKEN_ADMIN_ROLE for issuer-designated managers to authorize transfers
V3.3 audit reports listed (Oxorio Jan 29 2025, Sherlock Jan 22 2025, Certora Nov 07 2024, StErMi Oct 22 2024) but direct access to audit PDF details blocked

unknowns: D1: Etherscan name-tag systematic enumeration not completed; unable to confirm all Horizon-deployed contracts
D3-offchain: Audit PDF scope cannot be verified; GitHub audit folder at https://github.com/aave/aave-v3-horizon/tree/main/audits blocked from direct access

D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (Foundry broadcast/, hardhat-deploy/) not accessible; PoolAddressesProvider, PoolConfigurator, ACLManager, and other supporting contract addresses for Horizon instance not enumerated
D6: RwaATokenManager address not yet discovered; factory pattern unknown
D8: Only Pool address verified; RwaATokenManager and other admin/support contracts require either GitHub access or on-chain read-API queries

protocol_metadata.about: Aave Horizon RWA is a specialized Aave V3.3 lending market on Ethereum for institutional and qualified users to borrow permissionless stablecoins (USDC, GHO, RLUSD) against permissioned, tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) as collateral. Non-custodial smart contracts execute borrowing and lending deterministically. RWA issuers (Circle, Superstate, Centrifuge, etc.) manage whitelisting and KYC; aTokens are non-transferable by default to enforce compliance.
